response:
### Part 1: General Formula for Pages Read Each Day

We are given the sequence of pages read each day:

- Day 1: 5 pages  
- Day 2: 12 pages  
- Day 3: 21 pages  

This is a **quadratic sequence**, meaning the number of pages read on day $ n $ can be expressed as a quadratic function:

$$
P(n) = an^2 + bn + c
$$

We can use the three given values to solve for $ a $, $ b $, and $ c $.

#### Step 1: Set up equations

$$
\begin{align*}
P(1) &= a(1)^2 + b(1) + c = a + b + c = 5 \quad \text{(1)} \\
P(2) &= a(2)^2 + b(2) + c = 4a + 2b + c = 12 \quad \text{(2)} \\
P(3) &= a(3)^2 + b(3) + c = 9a + 3b + c = 21 \quad \text{(3)} \\
\end{align*}
$$

#### Step 2: Subtract equations to eliminate $ c $

Subtract equation (1) from equation (2):

$$
(4a + 2b + c) - (a + b + c) = 12 - 5 \Rightarrow 3a + b = 7 \quad \text{(4)}
$$

Subtract equation (2) from equation (3):

$$
(9a + 3b + c) - (4a + 2b + c) = 21 - 12 \Rightarrow 5a + b = 9 \quad \text{(5)}
$$

#### Step 3: Solve equations (4) and (5)

Subtract equation (4) from equation (5):

$$
(5a + b) - (3a + b) = 9 - 7 \Rightarrow 2a = 2 \Rightarrow a = 1
$$

Substitute $ a = 1 $ into equation (4):

$$
3(1) + b = 7 \Rightarrow b = 4
$$
